channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Mark Carney is sworn in as Canada's new prime minister as country deals with Trump's trade war
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Former central banker Mark Carney was sworn in as Canada’s new prime minister on Friday, and will now try to steer his country through a trade war brought by U.S. President Donald Trump, annexation threats and an expected federal election.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Aircraft catches fire after landing in Denver, sending passengers onto wing as smoke engulfs plane
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A fire on an American Airlines plane after it diverted mid-flight and landed at Denver International Airport sent fleeing passengers to a wing amid billowing clouds of smoke. Airport officials said 12 people were taken to hospitals with minor injuries.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS PEIPERT, SARAH BRUMFIELD, JESSE BEDAYN and COLEEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Voice of America ending contracts with Associated Press, Reuters and Agence France Presse
item.description: The government agency that runs the Voice of America has moved to terminate contracts with The Associated Press, Reuters and Agence France-Presse, and told its journalists Friday to stop using material from the wire services.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: International Delight coffee creamer shipped to 31 states is recalled
item.description: Danone, the distributor, issued a voluntary recall on some International Delight coffee creamers on Feb. 21 following reports of potential spoilage and illness related to the products' use.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hundreds of federal offices may start shutting down this summer due to DOGE
item.description: Federal agencies will begin to vacate hundreds of offices across the country this summer under a frenetic and error-riddled push by Elon Musk’s budget-cutting advisers to terminate leases that they say waste money.
item.dc:creator: By RYAN J. FOLEY, JOSHUA GOODMAN and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: At least 5 dead, including an infant, in 17-vehicle crash on I-35 in Texas
item.description: At least five people died and 11 were hurt in a crash involving at least 17 vehicles, including semi-trucks, on Interstate 35 in north Austin late Thursday night, police said.
item.dc:creator: Valentina Misas, NewsNation

item.title: Big March storm system threatens US with tornadoes, blizzards and wildfire risk
item.description: A huge storm system crossing the U.S. threatens to unleash tornadoes Friday in the Mississippi Valley, blizzards in the northern Plains and dry, gusty conditions in Texas and Oklahoma that pose a high risk of wildfires.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump's tariff wars forge rare bipartisan alliance in Kentucky as bourbon makers fear escalation
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — President Donald Trump's blunt-force use of tariffs has forged a rare bipartisan alliance among Kentucky's top leaders — Democratic Gov. Andy Beshear and Republican Sens. Mitch McConnell and Rand Paul — in lamenting brewing trade wars that could stagger the bourbon sector.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tesla says it is 'exposed' to retaliatory tariffs
item.description: Tesla warned U.S. trade representative (USTR) Jamieson Greer that the electric vehicle company and other American exporters are “inherently exposed” to retaliatory tariffs levied in response to ones from the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, WGN 9

item.title: Trump demands admissions overhaul and influence over academics at Columbia University
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration has delivered an extraordinary ultimatum to Columbia University, threatening to permanently end federal funding to the Ivy League school unless it cedes control of an international studies department and implements sweeping changes to other campus policies.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Protestant denominations cutting staff, trying new ideas amid declines in members and money
item.description: When the Episcopal Church recently announced cuts to its national staff, it was the latest in a long-running cycle among historic U.S. Protestant denominations — declines in members leading to declines in funding and thus in staff. And it wasn't alone. The Presbyterian Church (U.S.A.
item.dc:creator: PETER SMITH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 12 taken to hospital with minor injuries after plane catches fire at Denver airport
item.description: Twelve people were taken to hospitals after an American Airlines plane landed at Denver International Airport on Thursday and caught fire, prompting slides to be deployed so passengers could evacuate quickly.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, WGN 9

item.title: American Airlines Boeing 737 catches fire at Denver airport
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Twelve people were taken to hospitals after an American Airlines plane landed at Denver International Airport on Thursday and caught fire, prompting slides to be deployed so passengers could evacuate quickly.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Some student loan repayment plans have been suspended: What you should know
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration's recent changes to student loans are causing frustration and confusion for some borrowers.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Indiana officer gets unpaid leave after allegedly leaving child out in cold
item.description: Indiana police said a child in officer Cody Scott’s care was forced to stand outside in frigid temperatures with a sign that read, in part, “I want to stab and kill my brother.
item.dc:creator: Jenny Dreasler, NewsNation

item.title: Once-routine immigration check-ins become high-stakes calculation as some are detained
item.description: When a couple from Colombia who was planning their wedding showed up for a check-in with U.S. immigration authorities, one was given his next appointment date. The other was detained and deported. Jhojan doesn’t know why Felipe was detained at the Feb.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, CEDAR ATTANASIO and SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: VIDEO: American Airlines plane evacuated in Denver after fire aboard
item.description: Passengers were evacuated Thursday evening from an American Airlines flight after a fire was reported.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, KTLA 5

item.title: Trader Joe's recalls 61K sparkling water bottles
item.description: This week, Trader Joe's announced a recall of two lots of the popular Gerolsteiner Sparkling Natural Mineral Water, a non-Trader Joe's branded item, which the grocery giant sells. The recalled glass water bottles are being recalled over concerns they can crack, causing risk of cuts or slashes.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KTLA 5

item.title: Columbia University says it expelled some students who seized building last year
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Columbia University has expelled or suspended some students who took over a campus building during pro-Palestinian protests last spring and temporarily revoked the diplomas of others who have since graduated, officials said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Some universities are freezing hiring and laying off staff as Trump cuts federal funding
item.description: Universities across the U.S. have announced hiring freezes, citing new financial uncertainty as the Trump administration threatens a range of cuts to federal contracts and research grants. Some have announced layoffs.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Megastorm may bring blizzards, tornadoes, flooding and even fires across much of US: Where, when it will hit
item.description: More than 100 million people in the U.S. will be in the path of an intense March storm starting Friday as the sprawling multi-day system threatens fires, blizzards, tornadoes, and flooding as it tracks eastward across the Great Plains.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: March megastorm may bring blizzards, tornadoes, flooding and even fires across much of US
item.description: More than 100 million people in the U.S. will be in the path of an intense March storm starting Friday as the sprawling multi-day system threatens fires, blizzards, tornadoes, and flooding as it tracks eastward across the Great Plains.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Parents arrested after refusing child a ride to school; found walking on highway
item.description: The Midland Police Department arrested two people earlier this month after a young girl was found walking alone on a busy street in the early morning hours.
item.dc:creator: Gabriella Meza, WFLA, NBC

item.title: This common mixup is causing people to accidentally put glue in their eyes
item.description: Two very similar bottles stored in the same place in the home have led to a growing number of people seeking medical attention for accidentally putting glue in their eyes.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Saurer, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Federal student loan site down Wednesday, a day after layoffs gutted Education Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An hours-long outage Wednesday on StudentAid.gov, the federal website for student loans and financial aid, underscored the risks in rapidly gutting the Department of Education, as President Donald Trump aims to dismantle the agency.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Connecticut woman arrested after stepson allegedly held captive for over 20 years
item.description: A Connecticut woman accused of kidnapping a man and holding him captive for over 20 years was arrested Wednesday, police said.
item.dc:creator: Juliana Lepore, Fox 5

item.title: Lawsuit accuses man of killing, eating patients at SC mental health facility
item.description: The case against a South Carolina mental health facility where a man is accused of murdering and cannibalizing patients could be heading to trial.
item.dc:creator: Chase Laudenslager, WGN 9

item.title: The Scopes monkey trial took place 100 years ago, but the fight isn't over
item.description: NASHVILLE, Tenn. (AP) — They called it the “monkey trial.” It was supposed to be a publicity stunt. A hundred years later, it is remembered as far more. In March 1925, Tennessee became the first state in the country to ban the teaching of evolution in public school classrooms.
item.dc:creator: KIMBERLEE KRUESI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pete Buttigieg won't seek US Senate seat in Michigan, leaving door wide open for 2028 run
item.description: SAUGATUCK, Mich. (AP) — Former Transportation Secretary Pete Buttigieg announced that he will not seek an open U.S. Senate seat in Michigan, a move that leaves the door wide open for him to seek a much bigger role as his party’s next presidential nominee.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Family of NYC subway burning victim says Democrats need to answer for how migrant suspect allowed back into US
item.description: The family of a woman who police said was lit on fire and burned to death, said they want Democrats to answer about how the illegal migrant suspect got back into the country to commit the horrific crime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Explosion at Texas Tech causes evacuations, immediate spring break
item.description: An explosion at a substation on the Texas Tech campus has caused power outages at the Texas Tech and the Health Sciences Center Wednesday evening, according to a TechAlert.
item.dc:creator: Georgina Johnston, WGN 9

item.title: Indonesian fishermen sue Bumble Bee and say the canned tuna giant knew of abuse in its supply chain
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Lawyers representing four Indonesian fishermen who say they were beaten and trapped on vessels that were part of the global supply chain that provided tuna to Bumble Bee Seafoods filed a lawsuit Wednesday against the canned seafood giant.
item.dc:creator: JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: USDA ends program that helped schools serve food from local farmers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. Agriculture Department is ending two pandemic-era programs that provided more than $1 billion for schools and food banks to purchase food from local farmers and producers.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Virginia track athlete facing assault charge after baton strike she said was accidental
item.description: Alaila Everett said it was an accident but court records show she is now facing a potential assault and battery charge.
item.dc:creator: Kenzie Finch, WGN 9

item.title: Missing woman survives 6 days trapped in wrecked car as drivers pass unaware
item.description: For six days, Brieonna Cassell remained trapped inside her mangled Taurus as hundreds of cars passed by, unaware someone was in the vehicle.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, WFLA, NBC

item.title: PHOTOS: 132-year-old wreck of 'one of the safest ships afloat' discovered in Lake Superior
item.description: The Great Lakes Shipwreck Historical Society has announced the discovery of a new wreck on Lake Superior: The SS Western Reserve.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, Fox 5

item.title: Juarez police officer injured in body shop attack that left 3 dead
item.description: EL PASO, Texas (Border Report) – Three men were killed and two injured by gunfire during a shootout Sunday at a mechanical shop in Juarez, Mexico. Juarez police said at least two men arrived in a vehicle at the business in the Heroes de la Revolucion neighborhood around 7 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, Fox 5
